[An important feature of this algorithm is that when we resize the HashTable it grows](https://thinkpython.com/code/Circle.py) [geometrically;](http://docs.python.org/3/reference/datamodel.html#specialnames) [that is, we multiply the size by a constant.](http://tinyurl.com/thinkpython2e) [If you increase the size](https://thinkpython.com/code/has_duplicates.py) [arithmetically—adding a fixed number each time—the average time per](http://tinyurl.com/thinkpython2e) add [is linear.](https://thinkpython.com/code/has_duplicates.py)

[You can download my implementation of HashMap from](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sexagesimal) [https://thinkpython.com/](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sexagesimal) [code/Map.py](https://thinkpython.com/code/walk.py) [, but remember that there is no reason to use it; if you want a map, just use a](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Letter_frequencies) [Python dictionary.](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Letter_frequencies)

#### [B.5](http://www.cartalk.com/content/puzzlers) [Glossary](https://thinkpython.com/code/do_four.py)

[analysis of algorithms:](http://tinyurl.com/thinkpython2e) [A way to compare algorithms in terms of their run time and/or](http://tinyurl.com/thinkpython2e) [space requirements.](https://thinkpython.com/code/markov.py)

[machine model:](https://thinkpython.com/code/polygon.py) [A simplified representation of a computer used to describe algorithms.](https://thinkpython.com/code/polygon.py)

[worst case:](http://www.cartalk.com/content/puzzlers) [The input that makes a given algorithm run slowest (or require the most](http://www.cartalk.com/content/puzzlers) [space).](http://www.cartalk.com/content/puzzlers)

[leading term:](https://thinkpython.com/code/list_exercises.py) [In a polynomial, the term with the highest exponent.](http://docs.python.org/3/library/datetime.html)

[crossover point:](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Radix_sort) The problem size where two algorithms require the same run time or space.

[order of growth:](https://thinkpython.com/code/palindrome_soln.py) [A set of functions that all grow in a way considered equivalent for pur-](https://thinkpython.com/code/palindrome_soln.py) [poses of analysis of algorithms. For example, all functions that grow linearly belong](https://thinkpython.com/code/flower.py) [to the same order of growth.](https://thinkpython.com/code/flower.py)

[Big-Oh notation:](https://thinkpython.com/code/polygon.py) [Notation for representing an order of growth; for example,](https://thinkpython.com/code/polygon.py) [O](https://thinkpython.com/code/sed.py) [(](https://thinkpython.com/code/sed.py) [n](https://thinkpython.com/code/sed.py) [)](https://thinkpython.com/code/sed.py) [repre-](https://thinkpython.com/code/sed.py) [sents the set of functions that grow linearly.](https://thinkpython.com/code/pie.py)

[linear:](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Koch_snowflake) [An algorithm whose run time is proportional to problem size, at least for large](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Koch_snowflake) [problem sizes.](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Koch_snowflake)

[quadratic:](http://www.speech.cs.cmu.edu/cgi-bin/cmudict) [An algorithm whose run time is proportional to](https://thinkpython.com/code/reverse_pair.py) [n](https://thinkpython.com/code/reverse_pair.py) [, where](https://thinkpython.com/code/reverse_pair.py) [n](https://thinkpython.com/code/reverse_pair.py) [is a measure of](https://thinkpython.com/code/reverse_pair.py) [problem size.](https://thinkpython.com/code/reverse_pair.py)

[search:](http://wiki.python.org/moin/BitwiseOperators) [The problem of locating an element of a collection (like a list or dictionary) or](http://wiki.python.org/moin/BitwiseOperators) [determining that it is not present.](http://wiki.python.org/moin/BitwiseOperators)

[Appendix B. Analysis of Algorithms](https://thinkpython.com/code/interlock.py)

[hashtable:](https://thinkpython.com/code/polygon.py) [A data structure that represents a collection of key-value pairs and performs](https://thinkpython.com/code/letters.py) [search in constant time.](https://thinkpython.com/code/polygon.py)
